frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: forced-choice psychophysical thresholds index sensitivity to negative facial expressions

frameworks.name: basic emotions / universality
frameworks.one_line: view that facial configuration alone determines perceived affect

frameworks.name: BIS/BAS individual-difference framework
frameworks.one_line: approach/avoidance trait dimensions modulate context use in emotion perception

core_question: Does emotional context shift perceptual thresholds for detecting negative facial expressions, and do individual affective-processing traits modulate this contextual influence?
key_assumptions: perceptual sensitivity to facial emotion can be operationalised as a forced-choice psychophysical threshold
emotional contexts surrounding a face supply task-relevant affective information
BIS/BAS scores index stable individual differences in affective information processing
manipulations: emotional context surrounding target faces (varied across trials)
key_findings: contextual information shifted perceptual thresholds for negative facial expressions
the magnitude of context effects on facial-emotion perception varied with individual BIS/BAS affective-processing tendencies
interpretations: facial-emotion perception depends jointly on facial configuration and surrounding emotional context, not configuration alone
individual differences in affective information-processing style determine how strongly context is integrated into emotion judgments
